title: Burial Benefits Through the VA
summary: Veterans are often eligible for meaningful burial benefits that can ease both logistics and cost.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Burial Benefits Through the VA | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Veterans are often eligible for meaningful burial benefits that can ease both logistics and cost.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Eligible veterans can be buried in a national cemetery at no cost for the gravesite.
A burial allowance may help offset funeral costs.
A headstone or marker is typically provided at no cost.

paragraphs: Burial in a VA national cemetery is generally available at no cost to eligible veterans and often their spouse and dependents, and includes the gravesite, opening and closing, a liner, and perpetual care.

A separate burial allowance may help with funeral home costs, particularly if the death was service-connected, and a government headstone, marker, or medallion is typically provided regardless of burial location.

nextSteps: Ask the funeral home whether they handle VA burial benefit paperwork.
Confirm eligibility for national cemetery burial if desired.
Apply for the burial allowance and headstone benefit even if burial isn't in a VA cemetery.
disclaimer: General information — the VA can confirm specific eligibility and current benefit amounts.
